name: Polenta and Zucchini with Fresh Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 pound ripe plum tomatoes, quartered and seeded
4 medium zucchini (2 lbs.), cut lengthwise into half
recipeInstructions:
1. Several hours or overnight in advance, prepare polenta: Generously oil a 9- by 5-inch loaf pan.
2. In a 3-quart saucepan, bring water to a boil over high heat.
3. In a medium bowl, whisk together milk, cornmeal, basil, salt, and thyme until blended.
4. Slowly stir the mixture into boiling water, reducing heat to medium.
5. Cook, stirring frequently, until the polenta is very thick (about 5 minutes).
6. Transfer polenta to the oiled pan and let it cool. Cover and refrigerate until firm (about 2 hours or overnight).
7. Meanwhile, prepare fresh tomato sauce: In a blender, puree tomatoes and salt until smooth. Transfer to a pitcher; cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving.
8. Thirty minutes before serving, remove tomato sauce from refrigerator to come to room temperature. Preheat the grill to medium.
9. Coat the grill rack with nonstick cooking spray.
10. Loosen polenta from the pan using a knife, then unmold onto a cutting board and cut crosswise into 12 slices.
11. Brush polenta slices and zucchini halves lightly with oil.
12. Grill polenta and zucchini for 6 inches above heat until lightly browned (about 5 minutes).
13. Turn and grill the remaining side for 5 to 10 minutes, or until polenta is browned and zucchini is just cooked through.
14. Transfer polenta slices to a serving platter and zucchini to a cutting board.
15. In a medium bowl, combine remaining oil, vinegar, and salt. Toss zucchini in the mixture; arrange around the polenta.
16. Garnish with thyme sprigs and serve with tomato sauce.
